mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on equities and improves risk appetite, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when FEDFUNDS is trending down from an elevated level.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines at least 25 bps over the past 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 20-day returns more often than negative ones, or if the sign of the average 20-day return is not positive in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and earnings expectations, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and payrolls both expand, it implies broad final-demand strength that supports revenue growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the signal fails if either series is flat or contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-high-demand condition does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward testing, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in industrial production alongside stronger housing starts indicates easing real-economy capacity constraints and healthier cyclical activity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint moves in industrial production and housing starts are not followed by positive 60-day returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp inflation acceleration forces tighter policy expectations and compresses equity multiples, so a monthly CPI upturn above trend should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-upsurge episodes are followed by positive 20-day returns with frequency above 55%, or if the average forward return is not below zero in backtests after the trigger is activated.

mechanism_narrative: A steep drop in GDP growth reflects broad demand deterioration and earnings downgrades, so quarterly GDP weakness should map into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contraction quarters do not precede negative 60-day returns more often than not, or if the signal loses predictive value once revisions are accounted for in a rolling out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll gains slow while unemployment rises, labor-market deterioration is broad enough to hurt earnings expectations and equity returns; the combined deterioration should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration signal is not more negative than either series alone, or if triggered windows show positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A flat policy rate combined with falling inflation implies a real-rate easing impulse even without nominal cuts, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2 months while CPIAUCSL inflation slows for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation-plus-stable-policy regime does not produce positive 5-day returns above baseline, or if the signal becomes negative in more than one third of rolling 1-year windows.
